tasks.message: Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'string'.
tasks.directive: Type assignment mismatch. Walk through the source and target types field-by-field — most often a DTO factory built `{ a, b, c }` but the typed target requires `{ a, b, c, d }` (one missing field) or named the field differently (`expiration_date` vs `expiry`). Map field names against the canonical interface in `types/api.ts` (or the equivalent contract type) and patch the factory. Original: Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'string'.

tasks.message: Argument of type 'unknown' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
tasks.directive: Argument type does not match the parameter type. Two common causes: (a) you passed an object where the API expects a sub-property of it (e.g. `getMetaAndAssetCtxs()` returns `{ universe }`, but `fuzzyMatchEntity` expects the bare array — pass `.universe`); (b) the function returns `Promise<T>` and you forgot `await`, so a promise lands where `T` was wanted. Inspect the call site. Original: Argument of type 'unknown' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
